name: secrets-router Cross-Reference — Blog Post + Paper Citation
value: HIGH — directly maps the paper's abstract defense theory to a working implementation; boosts visibility for both works
notes: The paper (§9, Table 1) identifies 'Execution layer' and 'Provenance layer' as the ONLY defenses capable of observing concrete harmful artifacts. secrets-router (github.com/josephrobertlopez/secrets-router) is a production implementation of exactly this: opaque handles prevent secrets from entering LLM context windows, and CDP fill executes them only at the execution layer — never in the inference layer where regex guardrails operate. This is a concrete example of what the paper says defenses MUST do.

connection_to_paper.section_9_defense_table: secrets-router operates at the Execution layer and Provenance layer — the two layers the paper identifies as necessary for catching ZK pipelines (V2) and instantiated procedures
connection_to_paper.section_9_monoid_extractor: The paper proves regex guardrails are blind to MOD_p encodings; secrets-router sidesteps this entirely by keeping secrets out of the regex-checked context window via opaque handle substitution
connection_to_paper.threat_model_v2_zk_pipeline: V2 attack uses ZK pipeline to avoid LLM context visibility; secrets-router's opaque handle approach prevents credential leakage even if an LLM is compromised
connection_to_paper.defense_in_depth: The paper recommends all-layer defense; secrets-router adds a concrete credential-isolation layer that regex guardrails cannot provide

deliverables.title: Why secrets-router: the algebraic argument for execution-layer credential isolation
deliverables.description: ~800 word post on jrlopez.dev connecting secrets-router to the guardrails paper. Key argument: regex guardrails are provably blind to encoding attacks, so NEVER let secrets reach the regex-checked inference layer. secrets-router enforces this at the architectural level.
outline: 1-para hook: guardrail bypass is proven, not just empirical
The paper's defense table: only execution + provenance layers see concrete artifacts
What secrets-router does: opaque handles keep secrets out of context = out of guardrail scope
CDP fill = execution-layer enforcement (the layer the paper says you need)
2 concrete scenarios: attacker crafts MOD_p bypass payload; guardrail misses it; but credential never entered context so CDP never fires
CTA: read the paper (Zenodo DOI), star secrets-router, use both

deliverables.description: Add 1-paragraph 'Theoretical Background' section to secrets-router README citing the paper with Zenodo DOI

If paper not yet submitted: add secrets-router as a footnote or reference in §9.3 (Monoid Extractor as Audit Tool) as an example of execution-layer defense implementation. If already submitted: save for v2 / camera-ready.

post_actions: Share blog post on LinkedIn, Twitter/X, HackerNews (Show HN: I proved regex LLM guardrails are mathematically bypassable, here's the algebra + a defense tool)
Tag Andy Zou, Nicolas Papernot on Twitter when sharing — warms up endorsement ask
